title: Upload Board Email Attachments to File Column Easily

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Do you spend too much time manually entering data into your monday board? If so, you need to check out our new automation that developed by integrating monday.com with make.com

This automation will create items on your Monday.com board when you receive an email and it will automatically add the associated attachments to the “File” column.

This means that you can spend less time on data entry and more time focusing on the important things, like finding the best candidates for your open positions.

Check it out here:-https://www.facebook.com/mohamed.musadhiq.9/videos/1033153574503988/?idorvanity=559630067538170

image1366×589 54.7 KB

image1365×550 26.8 KB

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
And for those who do not want to go through the work in make or hire a consultant to do it, and get something they can implement across many boards quickly, check out Update Magic #1 in the app marketplace.

It includes this capability, and with the free version includes 100 Ops per month. Each run of the recipe uses one Op and copies up to three files, every additional three files is an additional Op - with no limit on the number of files. There is a limit of 150MB of files (which is fair because this is the maximum email size for most providers).

Paid plans share their Ops across all apps from the developer, so you get the benefits of economy of scale sooner if you need multiple apps.

Full disclosure, I am with the developer of the app.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sharing, We can also extract “From address” and check the attached file(Resume) valid or not by using Make. Can you do the same using your app?
